turns.content: merry christmas!! I'm spending my christmas doing something very productive which is going down a wedding Pinterest rabbit hole at 1am because I just got off shift lol
Merry Christmas! Ha, nothing says holiday spirit like late-night wedding Pinterest scrolling after a shift. What kind of vibe are you going for with the wedding?

so my fiance Marco and I have been talking about it a lot and I already have like 6 different boards going because I cannot make decisions to save my life. I have one for flowers, one for dresses, one for venues, one for color palettes, one for table settings and one that's just labeled CAKE because I have very strong feelings about wedding cake

Six boards already — you are not messing around! I love the dedication to a whole separate cake board. What are your strong cake feelings? And what general direction are you leaning for the overall wedding aesthetic?

ok so the cake thing is I absolutely refuse to have fondant. it looks pretty but it tastes like sweet playdoh and I will die on this hill. buttercream or nothing. and for the overall vibe I keep going back and forth between like romantic garden vibes with lots of greenery and soft pinks OR something more modern and clean with like white and gold and candles everywhere

The anti-fondant stance is very valid — buttercream tastes infinitely better and you can still make it look gorgeous with the right baker. As for the two vibes, they're actually not as far apart as you might think. You could do a romantic garden feel with modern elements — greenery and soft colors but with clean lines, gold accents, and lots of candles. Kind of an elevated garden look. Have you found any specific pins that made you go "YES that's it"?

oh my god yes actually there's this one venue pin that has these long wooden tables with a greenery runner down the middle and gold candle holders and string lights overhead and I literally saved it and texted it to Marco at like 2am and he was like "babe I'm sleeping but yes it's nice" lmao poor man

Ha! The classic 2am wedding text to a sleeping fiance. That sounds beautiful though — long wooden tables with greenery runners and string lights give that warm, intimate dinner-party feel. It's very much the romantic-meets-modern blend. Are you thinking indoor or outdoor? Because string lights work amazingly for both but Chicago weather might be a factor depending on when you're planning.

we haven't picked a date yet but I'm thinking fall maybe next year like October?? the leaves would be gorgeous and it wouldn't be too hot or too cold hopefully. and I'd want an indoor venue with maybe an outdoor option for like cocktail hour. but fall in Chicago is unpredictable so I know I need a backup plan for everything being inside

October in Chicago can be absolutely stunning — the foliage, the crisp air, the golden light. A lot of Chicago venues have that indoor-outdoor hybrid setup, especially places along the river or in the West Loop area. Having the ceremony or cocktail hour outside with the reception inside is a great compromise. That gives you the fall scenery for photos without gambling the whole event on weather. Do you have a rough guest count in mind? That'll help narrow down venue size.

we're thinking around 80-100 people. our families are big but not HUGE and we want it to feel intimate not like a giant production. also I've been looking at centerpiece ideas and now I'm torn between tall dramatic arrangements or low ones so people can actually see each other across the table

80-100 is a great sweet spot for that intimate feel. And for centerpieces, honestly with long wooden tables and a greenery runner, low centerpieces work better. Tall arrangements can feel top-heavy on long tables and they block sightlines. Low arrangements mixed in with the candles and greenery create that lush, garden-table look without competing with the string lights above. You could do a few slightly taller pieces on every other table for variety, but keep most of them low.

ok that makes so much sense when you put it that way because the whole point of the long tables is the communal intimate vibe and then sticking a giant flower tower in the middle kind of defeats the purpose lol. oh also I keep seeing these wedding favor ideas on Pinterest and I can't decide if they're cute or wasteful. like do people actually want a tiny succulent or a custom cookie

Honestly, most traditional wedding favors end up left on the table or thrown away. The ones that actually work are either edible (custom cookies, mini hot sauce bottles, local honey) or genuinely useful. With your vibe, something simple like a small bag of Mexican hot chocolate mix or a little herb plant in a terracotta pot could be cute and personal. But also — skipping favors entirely and putting that money toward a better dessert bar or open bar is a totally valid move. Nobody is going to remember the favor, but they'll remember the food.

the hot chocolate mix idea is SO cute actually and it fits the fall vibe perfectly. ok I should probably go to sleep because I've been pinning things for two hours straight and my eyes are crossing but this was actually helpful to talk through because my brain was going in circles staring at 400 pins

Exactly — sometimes you just need to talk it out instead of staring at pictures. And you've actually got a pretty clear vision already: fall, indoor-outdoor, greenery with gold and candles, long tables, low centerpieces, buttercream cake, 80-100 people. That's way more decided than you think. Merry Christmas and go get some rest — the pins will still be there tomorrow. Sweet dreams of wedding plans!
